meta.issuer_cn: 宇树科技股份有限公司

company.name_cn: 宇树科技股份有限公司
company.headquarters: Building 1, No. 88 Dongliu Road, Xixing Street, Binjiang District, Hangzhou, Zhejiang Province, China
company.industry: C3499 Other General Equipment Manufacturing Not Elsewhere Classified (High-Performance General-Purpose Robots / High-End Equipment)

products.name: H1 Humanoid Robot
products.specs: First self-developed humanoid robot launched August 2023; achieved world's first electric-drive backflip in place; running speed exceeding 5 m/s (world record for full-size humanoid robot); applications in scientific research, application development, education, cultural performance, intelligent services

products.name: G1 Humanoid Robot
products.specs: Launched 2024; achieved electric-drive side flip in place (March 2025); 100m obstacle race in 33.71 seconds; participated in CMG World Robot Competition mech combat arena as sole entry; applications in scientific research, industrial inspection, intelligent services

products.name: H2 Humanoid Robot
products.specs: Self-developed humanoid robot; participated in martial arts performance 'Wu BOT' alongside G1 units in early 2026; demonstrated millisecond-level cluster coordination

products.name: B Series Quadruped Robot
products.specs: Industry-grade product line; applications in scientific research, industrial inspection, emergency rescue, intelligent services, energy/chemical inspection, smart firefighting, smart city

products.name: Go Series Quadruped Robot
products.specs: Consumer-grade product line; Go1 achieved running speed of 4.7 m/s (2021 world record for similar-spec quadruped robots); A1 achieved 3.3 m/s maximum running speed (2020); cumulative sales exceeding 30,000 units during reporting period

products.name: Robot Components
products.specs: Includes: joint modules, dexterous hands, collaborative robotic arms, perception sensors, high-performance motors, reducers (planetary reducers), encoders, laser LiDAR, depth cameras, millimeter-wave radar, linear actuators, rotary actuators, PCBA modules

products.name: Embodied Intelligence Models
products.specs: Includes: Embodied Physical Intelligence Model (robot 'cerebellum' for high-stability full-body agile motion control); Embodied Large Model (robot 'cerebrum' for understanding, autonomous decision-making, task planning); WMA (World-Model-Action) large model; VLA (Vision-Language-Action) large model; reinforcement learning algorithms; motion control systems

shareholders_pre_ipo.name: Beijing Zhongguancun Science City Technology Growth Investment
Shanghai Kechuang (Shanghai Science and Technology Innovation Center No.2)

key_risks: Risk of technology breakthroughs and product innovation falling short of expectations: The high-performance general-purpose robot industry is one of the most complex and cutting-edge technology fields, integrating software/hardware algorithms, AI reinforcement learning, embodied large models, scene data analysis, motors/reducers/sensors and many other disciplines. Technology innovation is frequent, upfront investment is high, and uncertainty is large. The company's embodied large model technology has not yet been deployed at scale in robot products, and if key technical breakthroughs cannot be achieved, the large-scale application process for general-purpose robots will face significant uncertainty.

Risk of downstream large-scale commercial application falling short of expectations: Large-scale commercialization of humanoid robots still faces uncertainty. Current technical maturity in areas such as embodied large model generalization capability and dexterous hand precision/durability is limited. The consumer-level market for general-purpose robots has not yet formed rigid demand, the application ecosystem construction process and market cultivation cycle are long, and constraints from energy systems, real-time computing platforms, regulatory policies, industry standards, and social acceptance may delay commercialization.

Risk of escalating international trade friction and regulatory policies: International trade friction has created obstacles for Chinese companies in overseas markets. Since 2025, the US government's tariff policies have fluctuated, increasing uncertainty. Overseas revenue accounted for more than 35% of total revenue in each reporting period. If the US continues to impose unfavorable trade and tariff policies, or places the company on restricted procurement or export control lists, the company may be unable to maintain high overseas sales growth or may experience a decline in performance. Imported materials procured through domestic agents account for approximately 20% of total raw material purchases, and adverse changes in supply chain and overseas market control policies could negatively impact the company.

Risk of intensified industry competition and disorderly/unfair competition: Global tech giants are accelerating their deployment and startups are proliferating, intensifying market competition. Companies such as Tesla with large-scale production capacity and AI technology resources could rapidly reduce unit costs of humanoid robots like Optimus, directly intensifying price competition and putting pressure on the company's product pricing, market share, and profit margins. The competitive focus is shifting from hardware manufacturing to full-stack 'perception-decision-execution' technology.

Risk of corporate governance issues from the special voting rights mechanism: Before this offering, the actual controller Wang Xingxing controls 34.7630% of shares and, through the special voting rights arrangement, controls 68.7816% of total voting rights. After the offering, this will decrease to no more than 65.3090%. Under special circumstances, the interests of the actual controller may not be consistent with other shareholders, potentially harming minority shareholders.

Risk of high revenue growth rate decline and gross margin compression: From 2023 to 2025, main business revenue grew by 29.95%, 145.83%, and 335.31% (reviewed) year-on-year, with gross margins of 44.22%, 56.41%, and 60.27% (reviewed) respectively. Future intensified competition, technology upgrades, or product iteration could lead to slower revenue growth, product price declines due to homogeneous competition, or cost increases from new equipment depreciation, potentially causing gross margin decline and adversely affecting profitability.

Risk of key technology leakage and loss of core personnel: The company has developed proprietary core technologies in high-performance general-purpose robots. Increasing competition between enterprises and regions for talent is intensifying. If key core technology is leaked or core R&D team members depart, it will adversely affect the company's technological innovation capability, market competitiveness, and operating results.

Risk of rapid inventory growth and impairment losses: At the end of each reporting period, the company's inventory book values were RMB 77.5374 million, RMB 78.9577 million, RMB 140.5805 million, and RMB 278.0366 million, accounting for 24.12%, 24.20%, 11.07%, and 11.14% of current assets respectively, with continuous growth. If raw material prices, supply chain, or market conditions change adversely, the net realizable value of inventory may decline significantly, increasing impairment losses.

Risk of rapid accounts receivable growth and bad debt losses: At the end of each reporting period, accounts receivable balances were RMB 7.8467 million, RMB 11.0671 million, RMB 21.5948 million, and RMB 83.4565 million. If downstream customers' financial conditions deteriorate, there is a risk that accounts receivable cannot be recovered, adversely affecting operating results.

Risk of large additional investment and expenditure from fundraising projects: The fundraising investment projects will generate significant depreciation, amortization, and expense expenditures. If the industry development trend, market environment, and other factors change adversely, causing the fundraising projects to fail to achieve expected returns, the additional depreciation, amortization, and period expenses will adversely affect future operating results.

Risk of exchange rate fluctuations related to foreign currency settlement of overseas revenue: Overseas main business revenue accounted for 57.21%, 55.63%, 55.70%, and 39.20% of main business revenue in each reporting period, primarily settled in USD. If future USD/RMB exchange rate volatility increases or exchange rate policies change significantly, it will have some impact on the company's performance.

Risk of operating performance decline due to multiple factors: If the company's new technology development or new product promotion falls short of expectations, raw material costs rise, R&D investment intensity increases, or large share-based payment expenses from new equity incentive grants occur, and the company fails to take effective measures in a timely manner, the company will face the risk of a significant decline in operating performance or even losses.

Risk of social attention hotspot and market demand fluctuations: High-performance general-purpose robots as a frontier technology field have attracted broad attention. However, if progress in key technology breakthroughs, cost control, or scene applications slows, current market enthusiasm and capital attention may significantly decrease. Long-term, large-scale commercialization requires overcoming technology reliability, production costs, market acceptance, and legal/regulatory factors; demand fluctuations could directly affect capacity utilization and revenue realization.

Risk of large share-based payment expenses impacting future performance: The company confirmed share-based payment expenses of RMB 18.7226 million in 2022 and RMB 349.0655 million in 2025 related to capital increases in the equity incentive platform Shanghai Yuyi. Future equity incentive grants will generate additional share-based payment expenses that may have a significant impact on future operating results.

Star Market-specific risks: Star Market listed companies are characterized by large R&D investment, high operational risk, unstable performance, and high delisting risk, and investors face significant market risks.

projects.name: Intelligent Robot Model R&D Project
projects.focus: Continuous R&D investment in embodied intelligence models including physical intelligence models (cerebellum) and embodied large models (brain); building computing infrastructure, large-scale datasets, automated annotation mechanisms, and an intelligent development training platform covering data processing, model training, and validation feedback; advancing cloud-edge-end computing collaboration to support model training and deep learning.

projects.name: Robot Body R&D Project
projects.focus: R&D of high-power-density motors, high-power-density transmission systems, high-power-density drives, multi-scale lightweight structural components for legged robots, and robot centralized thermal control systems; consolidating physical performance and cost advantages of high-performance general-purpose robot products.

projects.name: New Intelligent Robot Product Development Project
projects.focus: Accelerating iteration of various types of robot products including humanoid robots and quadruped robots; enriching the product matrix; strengthening key R&D links such as overall architecture design, core component adaptation, functional performance test optimization, and process design to increase applicable application scenarios and strengthen market competitive barriers.

projects.name: Intelligent Robot Manufacturing Base Construction Project
projects.focus: Acquiring production base and production/testing equipment to solve space limitations and insufficient capacity; introducing automated assembly lines to improve precision assembly efficiency of humanoid and quadruped robot core components; configuring precision testing instruments for full-process product quality inspection; implementing intelligent warehousing systems; achieving data integration across production, inventory, and procurement to improve per-capita output efficiency and reduce reliance on leased facilities.
